caption: Bharata receives Rama's paaduka at Chitrakoot
scene: At Chitrakoot, Lakshmana hands Lord Rama's sandals (paaduka) to Bharata at Rama's instruction; Bharata, in royal robes, kneels weeping and receives them reverently to enthrone at Nandigram. Rama and Lakshmana are in exile as forest ascetics — matted hair (jatamudi) and bark garments (valkala), no crowns or royal finery. Sita in simple forest attire.
prompt: Traditional Tanjore (Thanjavur) style devotional painting, wide landscape 16:9: the scene at Chitrakoot in the forest — Lakshmana handing Lord Rama's golden sandals (paaduka) to Bharata, as Rama watches with a blessing gesture. CRUCIAL: Rama and Lakshmana are living in exile as forest ascetics — both have jatamukuta (piled-up matted hair / dreadlocks), wear valkala (rough bark-and-fiber forest garments), and have NO crowns, NO royal silk, NO ornate jewelry. Rama is still blue-skinned and radiant with a halo but dressed as a tapasvi. Sita stands beside them in simple modest forest attire. Bharata, dressed in royal robes, kneels weeping and receives the sandals reverently with both hands and bowed head. A forest hermitage setting with trees and rocks (NOT a palace). Rich jewel-tone colors, gold-leaf gilded ornamentation and halos, ornate arched temple border, serene rounded figures, flat decorative background, classical South Indian temple art, no text, no lettering.
